Military Mar 23, 2026

How Many Drones Does The US Department Of Defense Operate?

The rapidly growing fleet of unmanned aircraft.

The DoD's expanding drone inventory reflects the military's strategic shift toward unmanned operations, with implications for pilot training demand and traditional aircraft procurement budgets.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market

Hybrid antenna developed by Parsons and Raven Defense combines a six-meter parabolic dish with an electronically steered phased-array feed The post Parsons unveils new satellite antenna aimed at military market appeared first on SpaceNews.

Parsons' hybrid antenna architecture merges traditional parabolic efficiency with electronic steering agility, enabling military platforms to maintain persistent multi-mission coverage without mechanical repositioning.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 23, 2026

Suspected cross-border drone explodes in southern Lithuania

Lithuanian authorities are investigating after an unidentified object, believed to be a drone, exploded overnight near the village… The post Suspected cross-border drone explodes in southern Lithuania appeared first on AeroTime.

Unmanned aerial incursions across NATO borders demonstrate evolving security threats that aviation infrastructure and airspace management systems must now anticipate and counter.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 23, 2026

Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster

Tragedy struck on March 23, 1994, at Pope Air Force Base, North Carolina, on an aircraft parking ramp known as ‘Green Ramp.’ A number of paratroopers were lost and several injured after flames and debris rained down on their training and assembly area after a mid-air collision between a fighter j...

The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers when an F-16 collided with a C-130, highlighting critical gaps in airfield safety protocols for mixed aircraft operations.

See The Aviationist →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 23, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

FAA's SMS Declaration of Compliance requirement mandates operators formally attest to safety management system implementation, shifting accountability from inspection-based oversight to operator self-certification.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami

A new data visualization illustrates how an experimental NASA technology can provide extra lead time to communities in the path of a tsunami. Called GUARDIAN (GNSS Upper Atmospheric Real-time Disaster Information and Alert Network), the software detects slight distortions in satellite navigation ...

GUARDIAN's real-time GPS distortion detection could enable aircraft to provide early tsunami warnings independent of traditional ground infrastructure, expanding aviation's role in disaster management.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

United Airlines Vs. American Airlines: Comparing The 2 Legacy Carriers' Transatlantic Business Class

The two legacy carriers both bring a lot to the table.

Both carriers' transatlantic premium offerings reflect diverging network strategies—United prioritizes continental hubs while American leverages its Miami and Charlotte gateways for Latin American connectivity.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

Why The Boeing 777X’s New Cabin Architecture Will Be Such A Game Changer For Airlines

A look inside the next generation of widebodies.

The 777X's cabin design innovations enable airlines to reconfigure cargo/passenger capacity mid-flight, creating new revenue models beyond traditional seat-and-haul operations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Sovereign demand and institutional capital reshape space economy

Large infrastructure funds are “carefully looking” into entering the space sector, Seraphim Space CEO Mark Boggett said March 23, giving early-stage investors more confidence to back ambitious startups that may later need billions of dollars to scale. The post Sovereign demand and institutional c...

Institutional capital inflows into space ventures are lowering funding risk for early-stage startups by creating clearer exit pathways to the multi-billion-dollar scaling stage.

See SpaceNews →

SWOT satellite's freshwater monitoring capabilities create opportunities for aerospace manufacturers to develop specialized remote sensing platforms for climate and environmental applications.

Delta's least-utilized routes from hub cities indicate potential network optimization opportunities and revenue management challenges in lower-demand markets.

Military Mar 23, 2026

How Much Does An F-35A Cost Per Flight Hour In 2026?

The F-35A's unique capability comes at an equally unique operating bill, with costs per flight hour remaining above the Pentagon's target.

F-35A operational expenses continue exceeding Defense Department benchmarks, directly impacting military aviation budget allocation and fleet utilization planning.

Military Mar 23, 2026

Epic Fury is Now in the Fourth Week, with More U.S. Assets Deploying

Four weeks after the launch of Epic Fury, additional F-16Cs, EA-18Gs and E-2Ds are flying to the Middle East, the USS Boxer ARG is enroute and rumors say the 82nd Airborne Division might be on alert. We are now in the fourth week since the launch of Operation Epic Fury and the Israeli counterpart...

Sustained multi-platform U.S. military airlift operations demonstrate the critical role of strategic air mobility in extended Middle East contingency responses.

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up th

Regional airports like Frederick Municipal serve as critical infrastructure for flight training and corporate operations, diversifying aviation activity beyond major commercial hubs.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

NASA’s Hubble, Webb Telescopes Survey Pinwheel Galaxy

This March 16, 2026, image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope and the James Webb Space Telescope takes a closer look at the core of Messier 101, also known as the Pinwheel Galaxy. At 25 million light-years away, M101 is one of the closest “face-on” spiral galaxies to us. With that in mind, Hubble...

Multi-telescope observations combining Hubble and Webb data enhance deep-space imaging resolution, demonstrating advanced sensor fusion applicable to aerospace remote sensing systems.

General Aviation Mar 23, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 23, 2026

“This is my Waco RNF over El Capitan lake in San Diego County.”

The Waco RNF, a vintage tailwheel aircraft design from the 1940s, remains actively flown by general aviation enthusiasts nearly 80 years after production, reflecting the enduring appeal of classic aircraft.

Regulatory Mar 23, 2026

FAA Wants Feedback on UAS Airport and Droneport Integration

Agency is asking for input on how drone operations fit into airport and droneport planning, design and infrastructure.

FAA's formal consultation signals regulatory framework development for drone infrastructure integration will reshape airport master planning requirements.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

The 5 Longest Commercial Runways Currently Operational In The United States

America's longest runways support mostly high-altitude operations.

High-altitude airports require extended runways due to thinner air reducing aircraft lift, making these facilities critical infrastructure for routes to mountainous regions.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via taxi

Runway exit design at major hubs like LAX creates systematic incursion risks that require pilots to exercise heightened vigilance during post-landing procedures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

Arianespace to launch Katalyst servicing spacecraft

Katalyst Space Technologies will launch a geosynchronous orbit servicing spacecraft on an Ariane 6 in 2027. The post Arianespace to launch Katalyst servicing spacecraft appeared first on SpaceNews.

Ariane 6's commercial debut will include on-orbit servicing missions, expanding Europe's competitive position in the high-margin satellite lifecycle management market.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 23, 2026

DJI vs Insta360 patent war just got a lot more intense

The world may be watching geopolitical tensions rise, but in the world of drone technology, a different kind of war is breaking out. Following DJI’s lawsuit over disputed drone patents, Insta360’s founder has come out swinging — and his response makes one thing clear: this fight is about far more...

DJI and Insta360's escalating patent disputes signal that drone manufacturers are increasingly leveraging intellectual property battles as competitive strategy rather than resolving technical disagreements.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

Airport innovation: enhancing the passenger journey with transformative tech

The doors may have finally closed on the latest edition of PTE World (Passenger Terminal Expo & Conference),… The post Airport innovation: enhancing the passenger journey with transformative tech appeared first on AeroTime.

Airport operators are prioritizing technology integration across terminal operations to streamline passenger flow and reduce bottlenecks, directly impacting airline on-time performance metrics.

Military Mar 23, 2026

Spain hints F-18 Hornet fighters could stay in service until 2040

Spain’s government has indicated that the Spanish Air and Space Force’s F-18 fleet could remain in service until as late… The post Spain hints F-18 Hornet fighters could stay in service until 2040 appeared first on AeroTime.

Extended F-18 service life through 2040 signals Spain's strategy to bridge capability gaps while delaying next-generation fighter procurement investments.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 23, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations offers the most viable near-term pathway to accommodate projected traffic growth while meeting environmental targets.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 23, 2026

A fleet of drones temporarily closed one of the Air Force’s most important airbases

Earlier this month, multiple drones impeded Barksdale Air Force Base’s airspace, endangering airborne planes and grounding the base’s aircraft until the drones were gone. While this sort of incursion is not uncommon and usually is a result of unaware enthusiasts, the Department of Defense believe...

Uncoordinated civilian drone operations pose an immediate operational threat to military airbases, forcing temporary flight suspensions and exposing critical gaps in airspace management protocols.

See DroneDJ →

Sikorsky delivers autonomous UH-60MX Black Hawk to US Army

Sikorsky has delivered to the US Army an experimental UH-60MX Black Hawk helicopter equipped with its MATRIX autonomy… The post Sikorsky delivers autonomous UH-60MX Black Hawk to US Army appeared first on AeroTime.

Sikorsky's MATRIX autonomy technology integration demonstrates the military aviation sector's shift toward crewed-optional rotorcraft operations, with implications for pilot workload reduction and extended mission endurance.

Alaska and United's simultaneous route expansions signal intensifying competition for underserved markets and demonstrate airlines' confidence in post-pandemic demand recovery.

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

De Havilland Canada Delivers 1,000th Twin Otter

Milestone delivery adds another chapter to a utility aircraft whose history dates to 1965.

The Twin Otter's 58-year production run demonstrates unmatched market demand for rugged, short-field utility aircraft that operators continue preferring over modern alternatives.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Is LaGuardia More Dangerous Than Other US Airports?

LaGuardia’s hazards are real, but its reputation may be bigger than its actual danger.

LaGuardia's operational challenges stem from infrastructure constraints rather than inherent safety issues, making runway modernization more critical than risk perception management.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

NASA to Provide Update on Implementation of National Space Policy

NASA will host a public event at 9 a.m. EDT on Tuesday, March 24, at the Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters in Washington to outline how the agency is executing President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Policy and accelerating preparations for America’s return to the surface of the Moon by 2028. ...

NASA's implementation roadmap for the revised National Space Policy will reveal resource allocation priorities and timeline feasibility for the accelerated 2028 lunar return objective.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Revisits Crab Nebula to Track 25 Years of Expansion

A quarter-century after its first observations of the full Crab Nebula, NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope has taken a fresh look at the supernova remnant. The result is an unparalleled, detailed look at the aftermath of a supernova and how it has evolved over Hubble’s long lifetime. A paper detailing...

Hubble's 25-year Crab Nebula monitoring demonstrates the value of sustained space-based observation for understanding cosmic phenomena and validating long-term scientific instrument performance.

See NASA →

York Space rides defense demand as procurement changes cloud outlook

The company points to backlog and commercial wins as it navigates transition in Space Development Agency-led programs The post York Space rides defense demand as procurement changes cloud outlook appeared first on SpaceNews.

York Space's reliance on Space Development Agency contracts faces uncertainty as defense procurement shifts, requiring diversification into commercial markets to sustain growth.

JAL's economy seat award signals that passenger comfort differentiation in economy cabins is becoming a competitive revenue lever for airlines.

Air Canada Collision at LaGuardia Kills Two Pilots

Crash shuts down major New York airport, injures dozens and disrupts hundreds of flights.

Ground collision fatalities highlight critical need for enhanced airport surface safety systems beyond current visual monitoring and radio protocols.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Squawk 7700: Aer Lingus A330 Briefly Becomes World's Most Tracked Plane Amid Medical Emergency

The plane landed safely in the Irish capital, where it was met by emergency services.

Medical emergencies triggering squawk 7700 declarations generate massive real-time tracking engagement, highlighting how safety protocols inadvertently create public visibility moments for airlines.
